req.title: System-Admin updates document
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a document through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates: (1) authenticated system-admin can update a document via PUT /api/documents, (2) mandatory fields are validated, (3) an audit record is written on success, (4) HTTP 200/201 with entity id on success, (5) HTTP 400 on validation error, (6) HTTP 401 if unauthenticated, (7) HTTP 403 if role is forbidden. Test cases are derived using EP (valid/invalid partitions), BVA (field length boundaries), STC (document lifecycle state transitions), DT (auth+role decision combinations), and NEG (missing mandatory fields, wrong role, no token). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up their own document.

tests.name: test_admin_updates_document_success
preconditions: system-admin credentials are available via <admin_auth_token> fixture
a document exists, created via <create_document> fixture
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token
Create a document to be updated
Send PUT /api/documents/{id} with all mandatory fields populated
Capture the HTTP response
expected: Response body contains the document entity id
Audit record for the update action is written (verified via GET /api/audit)
trace: R-WEB-030: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_admin_updates_document_success(admin_auth_token, create_document, api_base_url, get_audit_record):
    doc_id = create_document(admin_auth_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_auth_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {
        "title": "Updated Title",
        "content": "Updated content body",
        "category": "HR"
    }
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/documents/{doc_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert str(doc_id) in str(body.get("id", "")) or body.get("id") == doc_id
    audit = get_audit_record(doc_id, action="update", token=admin_auth_token)
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record was not written after document update"

tests.name: test_update_document_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: system-admin credentials are available via <admin_auth_token> fixture
a document exists, created via <create_document> fixture
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token
Create a document to be updated
Send PUT /api/documents/{id} with the mandatory 'title' field omitted
Capture the HTTP response
expected: Response body contains a validation error message referencing the missing field
trace: R-WEB-030: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_update_document_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(admin_auth_token, create_document, api_base_url):
    doc_id = create_document(admin_auth_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_auth_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {
        "content": "Some content without a title",
        "category": "HR"
    }
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/documents/{doc_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "title" in error_text or "validation" in error_text or "required" in error_text

tests.name: test_update_document_title_at_max_boundary_length
preconditions: system-admin credentials are available via <admin_auth_token> fixture
a document exists, created via <create_document> fixture
maximum allowed title length is known via <max_title_length> fixture (default 255)
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token
Create a document to be updated
Send PUT /api/documents/{id} with title set to exactly max_title_length characters
Capture the HTTP response
expected: Response body contains the entity id
trace: R-WEB-030: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-030: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_update_document_title_at_max_boundary_length(admin_auth_token, create_document, api_base_url, max_title_length):
    doc_id = create_document(admin_auth_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_auth_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    boundary_title = "A" * max_title_length
    payload = {"title": boundary_title, "content": "Boundary test content", "category": "HR"}
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/documents/{doc_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201 at max boundary, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("id") is not None
    assert len(body.get("title", boundary_title)) == max_title_length

tests.name: test_update_document_title_exceeds_max_boundary_returns_400
preconditions: system-admin credentials are available via <admin_auth_token> fixture
a document exists, created via <create_document> fixture
maximum allowed title length is known via <max_title_length> fixture (default 255)
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token
Create a document to be updated
Send PUT /api/documents/{id} with title set to max_title_length + 1 characters
Capture the HTTP response
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing title length
trace: R-WEB-030: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_update_document_title_exceeds_max_boundary_returns_400(admin_auth_token, create_document, api_base_url, max_title_length):
    doc_id = create_document(admin_auth_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_auth_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    over_limit_title = "A" * (max_title_length + 1)
    payload = {"title": over_limit_title, "content": "Boundary test content", "category": "HR"}
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{api_base_url}/api/documents/{doc_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400 for title over max length, got {resp.status_code}"
    error_text = str(resp.json()).lower()
    assert "title" in error_text or "length" in error_text or "validation" in error_text

preconditions: a document exists, created via <create_document> fixture using admin token internally
actions: Send PUT /api/documents/{id} with a valid payload but NO Authorization header
Capture the HTTP response

tests.name: test_update_document_forbidden_role_returns_403
preconditions: a non-admin user token is available via <non_admin_auth_token> fixture (e.g., role:viewer)
a document exists, created via <create_document> fixture using admin token internally
actions: Obtain a token for a user with role:viewer (not admin)
Send PUT /api/documents/{id} with a valid payload using the viewer token
Capture the HTTP response

tests.name: test_update_document_state_transition_draft_to_published
preconditions: system-admin credentials are available via <admin_auth_token> fixture
a document in DRAFT state exists, created via <create_document> fixture with status=draft
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token
Create a document in DRAFT state
Send PUT /api/documents/{id} with status set to 'published' and all mandatory fields
Capture the HTTP response
Fetch the updated document via GET /api/documents/{id} to confirm state
expected: Response body entity id is present
Subsequent GET returns document with status 'published'
Audit record for the state transition is written
trace: R-WEB-030: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_update_document_state_transition_draft_to_published(admin_auth_token, create_document, api_base_url, get_audit_record):
    doc_id = create_document(admin_auth_token, status="draft")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_auth_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = {"title": "Published Doc", "content": "Now published", "category": "HR", "status": "published"}
    resp = requests.put(f"{api_base_url}/api/documents/{doc_id}", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}"
    assert resp.json().get("id") is not None
    get_resp = requests.get(f"{api_base_url}/api/documents/{doc_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json().get("status") == "published"
    audit = get_audit_record(doc_id, action="update", token=admin_auth_token)
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record missing for state transition"
